title: Minuartia orthophylla
intro: Minuartia orthophylla, also known as the mountain sandwort, is a low-growing, succulent perennial plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Minuartia orthophylla include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Minuartia orthophylla is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve to treat a range of health conditions, including eczema, acne, and bronchitis.
